method: INTRA-WORD SIGN REPETITION (reduplication vs anti-repetition; L2/L3). rep_rate = fraction of len>=2 words containing >=1 repeated sign (any sign appearing >=2x within the word). FROZEN NULL = i.i.d.-unigram, LENGTH-MATCHED: for each observed word of length L, draw L signs i.i.d. from the GLOBAL unigram frequency distribution; compute rep_rate over the synthetic corpus; >=2000 draws; TWO-SIDED p for deviation; direction = excess (reduplication) / deficit (avoidance). Tests whether words repeat signs more/less than expected from sign frequencies + word lengths alone. POSITIVE CONTROL on Linear B (DAMOS) gates the verdict: DETECT (plant injected-reduplication EXCESS -> detected as excess p<=0.05; AND plant all-repeat-words-removed DEFICIT -> detected as deficit p<=0.05) AND false-positive control (words generated BY the i.i.d.-unigram model = true H0; rejection rate <=0.10 across >=20 sets). Cross-site held-out per site with >=50 words; leave-one-site-out on Haghia Triada. Pure L2/L3 statistics on ANONYMOUS sign ids; no phonetics/sound/meaning/reading. 'repetition' is a distributional statistic, NOT a claim about sound or morpheme meaning.
result: Linear A intra-word sign repetition is CONSISTENT WITH CHANCE under the length-matched i.i.d.-unigram null. The observed rep_rate (0.0709, 97/1369 len>=2 words with >=1 repeated sign) is essentially identical to the null expectation (mean 0.0735; two-sided p=0.74, direction a negligible deficit). There is therefore NO evidence for either systematic reduplication (excess) or systematic anti-repetition (avoidance): once sign frequencies and word lengths are accounted for, words repeat signs at exactly the rate expected from random sign sampling. The positive control PASSED (detects planted excess p=0.00 and planted deficit p=0.00 with correct directions; false-positive rate 0.10 <= 0.10 on true-H0 synthetic words), so the null result is informative rather than a machinery failure. Per-site, only Haghia Triada reaches significance (deficit, p=0.002) while the other 5 testable sites are non-significant and the leave-one-site-out (excluding Haghia Triada) is non-significant (p=0.487) — i.e. the single significant site does not survive LOO and the global signal is null. Verdict: REPETITION_AT_CHANCE.
key_findings: GLOBAL: observed rep_rate = 0.0709 (97/1369 len>=2 words contain a repeated sign) vs i.i.d.-unigram length-matched null mean 0.0735; two-sided p = 0.74, direction a negligible deficit. Repetition is CONSISTENT WITH CHANCE.

POSITIVE CONTROL PASSED on Linear B (DAMOS): planted reduplication excess detected (p=0.00, direction excess), planted all-repeat-removed deficit detected (p=0.00, direction deficit), and false-positive rate = 0.10 (2/20 true-H0 synthetic sets rejected) <= 0.10 gate. The null AT_CHANCE result is therefore informative, not a machinery failure.

CROSS-SITE: 6 sites have >=50 words (Haghia Triada, Zakros, Khania, Knossos, Phaistos, Palaikastro). Only Haghia Triada is individually significant (deficit, p=0.002); the other 5 are non-significant (p in [0.07, 1.0]) and Palaikastro trends excess. Direction is NOT consistent across sites.
LEAVE-ONE-SITE-OUT (excluding Haghia Triada, the largest site): rep_rate 0.1081 vs null 0.1000, p=0.487, non-significant. The single significant site does NOT survive LOO, confirming the global null.

The length-matched i.i.d.-unigram null is the right baseline: ~45% of words are length 2, where the analytic P(repeat)=sum p_i^2 ~ 0.023 already accounts for most of the expected repetition; observed length-2 rep_rate (0.0113) is in fact slightly BELOW this analytic expectation, consistent with the tiny global deficit that is not significant.

No evidence for systematic reduplication (a morphological process producing excess repeats) NOR for systematic anti-repetition (a phonotactic/orthographic constraint producing deficit). Repetition structure is fully explained by sign frequencies + word lengths.
successor_hypotheses: E041: Test POSITIONAL repetition — are repeats concentrated at specific positions (e.g. word-initial reduplication A-A-X, or word-final) rather than anywhere in the word? A positional test could reveal reduplication that the position-agnostic rep_rate averages out.

E042: Test ADJACENT vs non-adjacent repetition separately — true morphological reduplication is typically ADJACENT (immediately repeated sign); the current metric counts any repeat. An adjacency-restricted metric may detect a signal hidden in the aggregate.

E043: Test whether the Haghia Triada deficit (p=0.002) is a genuine site-specific anti-repetition constraint or an artifact of its length distribution / sign inventory; replicate on a Haghia-Triada-only length-matched null with site-local unigram.
E044: Test BIGRAM repetition / sign-pair recurrence rather than single-sign repetition — words may avoid or favor repeated bigrams (X-Y ... X-Y) even when single-sign repetition is at chance.

E045: Cross-script comparison — compute the same rep_rate + length-matched i.i.d. null on Linear B (DAMOS) and other syllabic corpora to establish whether AT_CHANCE is the typological norm or whether Linear A is unusual; this calibrates the null result.

E046: Test CONDITIONAL repetition by word length — does the deficit/excess direction flip across length bands (e.g. excess in long words, deficit in short)? A length-conditional analysis may reveal structure invisible to the pooled rep_rate.
non_circular: Signs are ANONYMOUS tokens throughout — no phonetic value, sound, meaning, or reading is assigned or used. The metric (rep_rate) and null (i.i.d.-unigram, length-matched) are pure L2/L3 distributional statistics over sign identity within words. Linear B (DAMOS) is used ONLY as a positive-control benchmark to validate the machinery (detect planted excess/deficit, control false positives); it is never the target of the Linear A claim. The verdict is computed mechanically from the frozen rule in prereg.md by the verdict() function in machinery.py; the operator does not adjudicate. No phonetic/sound/meaning/reading inference is made: 'repetition' is a distributional statistic, not a claim about morpheme meaning or sound.
deviations: The vectorized null implementation initially contained a padding-sentinel bug (padding positions counted as distinct values, inflating distinct counts and suppressing detected repeats, yielding a spurious strong-excess signal). The bug was caught by an independent analytic check (P(repeat|L=2) = sum p_i^2 ~ 0.023 vs the buggy null ~0.0047) and a brute-force pure-python null (mean 0.0739). The null was rewritten to group words by length and draw (n_draws, count_L, L) matrices with NO padding, matching the analytic and brute-force references (corrected null mean 0.0735). The frozen METRIC and NULL definition in prereg.md were NOT changed; only the implementation was corrected to faithfully realize the frozen definition. The corrected result (AT_CHANCE) supersedes the buggy result.
PC false-positive rate landed exactly at the gate boundary (0.10 = 2/20). This passes the <=0.10 rule as written; noted for transparency. A larger FP set count would refine this estimate.
